title: Cables, Connectors, Adapters, Interfaces, and Capability Negotiation · Mastery Check
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for cables, connectors, adapters, interfaces, and capability negotiation and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for connector and end-to-end capability map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.
Compare category, conductor, shielding, length, bend, rating, directionality, active components, markings, and environmental suitability.
Distinguish passive pin adaptation from active protocol conversion and account for direction, resolution, bandwidth, audio, copy protection, and power.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Signal Path Test, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Signal Path Test precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Signal Path Test, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Signal Path Test, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Replacing every cable and adapter simultaneously is the fastest way to identify cause.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and signal plus specific case evidence.
